text:
TheParis Olympicshas sparked a remarkable increase in Tinder activity in the city of love. The competition hascaptivated global attentionand data from Tinder has shown how the Olympics has influenced the dating scene in the French capital.

While the Olympic Village can ignite relationships, there are other avenues for the more than 10,000 Olympians to forge new connections. Before the official start of the Olympics, dating app Tinder experienced a 20 per cent surge in swipe activity across France, compared to last July.

There will always be people on the quest for love and other potential reasons for a sudden spike in Tinder's popularity. However, a closer look at the data reveals a distinct Olympic theme, with a flood of fans from around the world also heading to Paris tosupport their country's leading stars.



From 1 to 25 July, the number of Tinder users listing Olympian as their profession rose by 52 per cent, while there was a 43 per cent increase in people listed as athletes on the app. Olympic stars would also have had the opportunity to scout out potential partners on Tinder before flying out to the Paris Games.

Tinder's Passport Mode, which allows users to switch their location to Paris and message locals, saw a staggering 103 per cent increase in activity around key tourist areas in Paris during July. The USA, Brazil and Turkey ranked as the top three countries where people changed their location to France during the same period in July.

In addition, the Olympics have sparked a surge of Tinder users expressing their passion for sports. Gymnastics is the fastest-growing sport on the app, with a 31 per cent increase in people listing it as an interest last month.

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

Football, basketball and swimming are the top three most popular sports on the app, with basketball seeing a 14 per cent rise in interest. Tinder is accustomed to a summer surge in usage as people travel to new destinations.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

The data reveals that Passport Mode is used an average of 145,000 times per day, while those in the 18-25 age group using the feature send and receive three times more messages than those not using it.

However, for any Olympic star needing extra privacy, incognito mode allows users to swipe normally, but only those they have liked will see them in their recommendations.

title: Tinder data in Paris shows Olympians eyeing love at Games | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Jake Bayliss
description: The Paris Olympics has had a notable impact on the dating scene in France and athletes could be among those looking for love, according to data from Tinder.
